item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: This is the greatest SCP that is based around a city! This really has its own universe and we can really feel like it has some kind of mystery.

item.title: 2007 Book?
item.content:encoded: It’s always been a little weird to me that this SCP seems more well known than China Miéville’s book Un Lun Dun about a place also known as “UnLondon.” The narratives aren’t similar at all, it’s really just the name, but this always comes up before the book when I do searches. Maybe it’s my search engine, maybe it’s just that SCP is really popular and the book is sorta niche, but I kinda wish more people knew the book. It’s really good.

item.title: Always been a sucker for a good dystopia
item.content:encoded:
Unlondon honestly doesn't seem like a bad alternative to whatever apocalypse the world actually ending from anomalies would cause. If this was a game or a video series, I'd watch it.

+1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
It's sort of like Fallen London.

Maybe Fallen London is UnLondon, and that's why there are so many stories about people not being able to leave to the surface. It's propaganda meant to dissuade attempts to surface.

item.title: Don't Question it.
item.content:encoded:
+ show block
– hide block

We're gonna build a whole new world for ourselves. Look, they clap eyes on us and we're dead, right?
So we gotta make a new life where they'll never find us. You know where? Underground!
You should see it down there - hundreds of miles of drains - sweet and clean now after the rain!
Dark, quiet, safe. We can build houses and everything, start again from scratch!
And what's so bad about living underground eh? It's not been so great living up here, if you want my opinion!

[Verse 1]
Take a look around you at the world we've come to know
Does it seem to be much more than a crazy circus show?
But maybe from the madness something beautiful will grow

[Chorus]
In a brave new world
With just a handful of men
We'll start-
We'll start all over again!
All over again!
All over again!
All over again!

We'll build shops and hospitals and barracks right under their noses - right under their feet!
Everything we need - banks, prisons and schools… We'll send scouting parties to collect books and stuff, and men like you'll teach the kids
Not poems and rubbish - science
So we can get everything working
We'll build villages and towns and… and… we'll play each other at cricket!
Listen, maybe one day we'll capture one of those Broken God things, eh? Learn how to make 'em ourselves and then wallop!
Our turn to do some wiping out! Whoosh with our Death Ray - Whoosh!
And them running and dying, beaten at their own game
Humanity on top again!

[Verse 2]
Now our domination of the Earth is fading fast
And out of the confusion the chance has come at last
To build a better future from the ashes of the past
In a brave new world

[Chorus]
Give me a handful of men
We'll start all over again

[Verse 3]
Look - Man is born in freedom but he soon becomes a slave
In cages of convention from the cradle to the grave
The weak fall by the wayside but the strong will be saved
In a brave new world

[Chorus]
Give me a handful of men
We'll start all over again

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Imagine the people behind Bioshock making a game out of this. It has all the workings of a great game, perhaps someone more talented than me will follow it through.

item.title: Junior Staff Post
item.content:encoded:
Posting here for clarity.

The second image originates from here. Staff had contacted the creator five years ago, and as far as we know, we never got a response back. As part of Licensing Staff, I will assume the author does not give permission to use this image, as they own all images under a copyright, and thus assume it is not compliant with CC 3.0. As such, the second image will most likely be removed/replaced.

Edit: due to being extremely overdue, Licensing Staff has decided to remove the second image. A replacement may be provided after staff discussion.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I love this piece thoroughly! If for nothing else, I'd have upvoted it for the speech alone. But there's so much about this I love that I can't vote for it enough.

++good!

As a sidenote, the speech reminds me a lot of this one, which is incidentally my favourite one of all time.

item.title: Underrated SCP
item.content:encoded: This SCP file is one of my favourites if not my very first favourite. It is a shame there is little fan art of it and we will never truly know what lies within the parliament building of UnLondon.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
“The sky has broken,”
I guess during the events of SCP-001 When Day Breaks, UnLondon will serve it’s purpose. Loved the reference to Cortex Leeches as well.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
SORRY WESTRIN, this was a response to the original author and now I can't delete the message

Really loved the writing, I wish there could be more!

My only complaint is that at the end it states that "It is unclear as to what event or disaster SCP-1678 is being prepared for." and yet the museum exhibit "The fall of Man" has representations of many different SCP's depicted in them in "apocalyptic settings". I feel they contradict between them a bit since the exhibit seems kinda self-explanatory as to why the city was built.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I know I'm 6 months late, but I like the new image a lot more than the original.

item.title: City of London != London
item.content:encoded: The city of london is a small city within the boarders of the city called london, but it's still technicaly a different city.Technicality yes, but a distinction that I feel the SCP foundation would try to make.

item.title: Re: Staff Post - Info - Open
item.content:encoded: Oh, cool! No problem! - I'm glad you liked it. It felt like such a shame (although from the artists perspective, understandable) for Unlondon to have no visual representation for it's most iconic entity.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: While I liked the classic image, I'm glad it was removed as an artist myself, I would be unhappy to have my art used without permission. That and said artist probably gets flooded with comments about 1678 ^^;. The new image fits a lot better, with the older police uniform. The previous image always left me thinking that 1678-A dressed a lot like military officers.

item.title: Re: Staff Post - Info - Open
item.content:encoded: After discussion amongst staff, your picture has been added to the article. Thank you very much for creating and volunteering it!

item.title: Staff Post- Image Replacement
item.content:encoded:
The original image has been replaced with a new one graciously created by user VolgunStrife. The new image is a modified version of this image of a Victorian Constable, which was licensed under cc 3.0.

Posting as a reply to the top comment for user visibility.

item.title: Re: Staff Post - Info - Open
item.content:encoded: Yeah that picture is much better, hopefully this'll go up in the article.

item.title: Re: Staff Post - Info - Open
item.content:encoded: Wow, honestly that's much better than the original imo. Nice work Volgun!

item.title: Re: Staff Post - Info - Open
item.content:encoded:
I was informed of this, too.

I have prepared a replacement image that is within the wikis guidelines.

The image of the police officer is under creative commons, here it is if you are curious).

item.title: Staff Post - Info - Open
item.content:encoded: This is a notification that one the images used in this article has been taken down due to license incompatibility. The image originates from a deviantart here, which violates our Licensing rules and Image Use Policy and has been removed. The artist in question has specifically requested its removal. You are not currently in any trouble for this, but please be more careful in the future to only use public domain or other CC-by-SA 3.0 compatible images in your articles. If you have any questions about this decision or our policy, please feel free to ask.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: This SCP is fairly well known in the off-site fandom due to it's rather iconic image, and the fantastic imagery within the SCP.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Out of curiosity, what publicity are you talking about?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Love this one. In hindsight I feel this SCP works even better as the site grows since it sounds like some sort of twisted version of a Thaumiel SCP.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I could definitely imagine a Bioshock game taking place in this setting. It feels very Shock-ey, in a good way.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: given the level of publicity this one has outside the site im surprised the score isn't higher.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Very well done!
My only complaint is that there isn't more!
I'd LOVE to see more exploration logs, these kind of SCPs tickle me in all the right ways.
+1.

item.title: Re: Plagiarism?
item.content:encoded: Depends. Are you saying that this similar to UnLondon from Un Lon Dun, or is it exactly the same as UnLondon from Un Lon Don, complete with the officers and inhabitants?

item.title: Plagiarism?
item.content:encoded: UnLondon is the name of the “abcity” in China Mieville’s book Un Lun Dun. It is very similar in structure (a mirror of London) and this whole article feels very offputting to me because of that. Can anything be done on this?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Amazingly detailed, subtly terrifying, and has just the right influences. Upvote, and one of my favorites
EDIT: I just noticed they warned of "the Slaver Man". What is that, exactly?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: This is amazing. Well written and draws from a lot of concepts I really like. The only bad thing about it, is that it isn't explored further. Makes me really want write a tale.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Derivative of what? Certainly for an article that's "extremely derivative", with "nothing… to justify its existence", one could attribute almost all of the plot elements, and their synthesis, to already published works.

If you're just trying to say that you've seen stuff like this elsewhere… that can be a dealbreaker if you want it to, but get used to people disagreeing. If the entire wiki was held to these standards of originality, we'd have about half as many articles.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Extremely derivative with nothing in its execution or style to justify its existence.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Actually, Gaiman meets World War II.

Or Victoria meets Grant Morrison.

There are many, many vectors by which this can be arrived at. That does not matter. What matters is the execution.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Mielville meets Sunless Sea. Yawn.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I was wondering if anyone would ever use that picture for anything interesting.
I wonder what SCPs specifically are referred to in the displays in the museum? I hope the foundation wrote them down somewhere so they make extra special sure to keep those SCPs under control! Maybe they should have a seperate document that this one can refer to for that?

Either way, I like how elaborate this one is. Would there be any way I could get to "UnLondon" through any of the neglected London Underground tunnels that are out of commission? We find a lot of things down there.

How did they find 1678? Through a manhole?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: The brand ‘Dr. Goody’s Wonderfood’ reminded me to Wondertainment. I canon the creator of this product and the whole SCP as a member of the Wondertainment family, going paranoid about the Foundation failing in containing stuff and creating a shelter with the anomalous technology used on their toys.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Brilliant, awesome concept, wonderfully executed, and a nice homage to a certain underwater city. UPVOTED.

Seriously, though, this is a pretty underrated SCP. It was one of my favourites when I lurked on the site.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: ALL dress uniforms, when properly worn, are hot. There's no such thing as an ugly dress uniform.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
The image of SCP-1678-A is damn hot. I love this.

(+ Opps, I've forgot that I have already commented in July, 2014. Sorry for unnecessary repetition)

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: This really is a good article, which is why I'm writing my first comment on the site for it. However, I have a nitpick that's been bothering me. The article talks about a "Fall of Man" exhibition in UnLondon's Natural History Museum. Presumably this shows the progress of the apocalyptic event that led to the creation of UnLondon as a place of refuge. I think that's a nice idea, but one problem: the NHM is solely concerned with NATURAL history, and has nothing to do with human civilisation. So it would make much more sense for the exhibition to be in one of the scores of museums in London that DO concern themselves with human civilisation and history. I'd suggest the British Museum as it's old enough to be viable (pre-dating Victorian London, in fact) and well-known enough to be recognised by most readers. It's of minor importance, I know, but even allowing for the ongoing apocalypse I find it jarring that the Londoners apparently erected an exhibition in the wrong museum.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Wow! I really like this SCP! I especially liked the whole idea with the museum displaying 'The Fall of Man'! Awesome idea!
I can already picture a great hall containing paintings and sculptures of horrific scenes, and maybe a replica of SCP-173 in a glass case XD

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Fascinated by the image of 1678-A.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Fantastic. +1.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Finally! another Warhammer player on this website! Thankyou Cryokina!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Noone likes Batman anyway - If it were anything like Batman, I would have downvoted. I didn't.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: It does resemble Orwell's vision of London-and seems to have a steam-punk root. I can't help thinking of Down and out in Paris and London.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I like the idea behind this a lot, but some of the execution is poor (e.g. it should be 'bobby' in singular and actually it shouldn't be used at all because it's too modern to fit the rest of the SCP; also, their uniform is totally wrong) and it just feels… off, somehow. Maybe it's meant to be like that?

It doesn't feel like its creator (both in-universe and out-) has ever lived in London. That's what's feeling off, for me.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: It's derivative sure, but I can't help but like this SCP. Dystopian/post apocalyptic concepts are some of my favorites, this synthesizes a bunch of them into a compelling whole and tosses in a healthy dose of steampunk. If someone were to make a sandbox PC game out of this I'd play the hell out of it. You got my vote.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Ye Olde Fallout Vault, i liked this one, good job

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Then again, if you open the picture full-size, there's a graffiti on the wall to the right. In German.
Maybe this London is not really London…

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: An amusing entry. I'm thrown off a bit by the bobby being portrayed by Kroenen from Hellboy, but I could see how it could come about. Faceless is intimidating and the German have had a reputation for ruthless efficiency in more recent years (the older stereotype basically portrayed Germans as lazy beer-swilling art-lovers), so I could see where they'd wind up with someone similar.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Yes. I just read this and these problems stood out to me as well.

The author is lucky the rest of this article is so blood brilliant, as inconsistency is one of my major pet peeves when it comes to writing fiction.

I dunno if Astronaut Joe is still active, but if he's out there, I hope he sees this and fixes this inconsistency somehow. Otherwise, this is really wonderful work. It would be a shame for something so simple (and at the very end!) to hold it back from becoming a classic.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
As one of our readers said:

heavily enriched with various minerals, vitamins, fats and bulking agents

contradicts not only

due to low levels of fat

but

illnesses such as scurvy

as well, as scurvy is essentially Vitamin C deficit.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Started to look like Proffesor Layton and the Unwound Future, and then changed its mind and became Bioshock. Though I don't see it as an original pece of work, I still enjoyed reading throughout this article. +1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I like the idea of this, but as people have repeatedly pointed out, there's not a particularly interesting spin on the whole concept. The idea of buildings degrading over distance and all that that entails would make for a far more interesting article than Rapture-But-London.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: It's Vault-Tec meets Rapture. I don't find this particularly compelling.

item.title: is anyone able to program this?
item.content:encoded:
i just need to say that this would be pretty awesome, if put in a game like scp-cb.
-random maps with large bug-like anomalys
-hard to kill but not never invincible enemies
-light and dark areas equally

but i unfortunately dont know how to program so…

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: yeah maybe with this expander-ball 184 that would explain the errors further away from the rough center.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Definitely my favorite SCP, mainly because I think this could be one the Foundation is responsible for.

Remember the Document Recovered From The Marianas Trench? It implied that SCPs had broken out of containment before and decimated the planet, and the Foundation was able to get everything back up and running with memories and records of the Apocalypse wiped. But what if they missed something in one such case? What if, during one such outbreak, someone set up Unlondon? Probably through the use of some other SCP. And maybe when the world got rebooted, so to speak, memory of the city was wiped but Unlondon itself was left intact and running.
